<content>1: // ─── SSE Event Wiring ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // Extracted from relay-stack.ts: the pipeline that takes SSE events from
3: // OpenCode, translates them, filters by session, records to cache, broadcasts
4: // to browser clients, and sends push notifications.
5: 
6: import type { Translator } from "./event-translator.js";
7: import type { MessageCache } from "./message-cache.js";
8: import {
9: 	hasInfoWithSessionID,
10: 	hasPartWithSessionID,
11: 	hasSessionID,
12: 	isPermissionRepliedEvent,
13: 	isSessionErrorEvent,
14: } from "./opencode-events.js";
15: import type { PermissionBridge } from "./permission-bridge.js";
16: import type { PushNotificationManager } from "./push.js";
17: import type { QuestionBridge } from "./question-bridge.js";
18: import type { SessionManager } from "./session-manager.js";
19: import type { SessionOverrides } from "./session-overrides.js";
20: import type { SSEConsumer } from "./sse-consumer.js";
21: import type { OpenCodeEvent, RelayMessage } from "./types.js";
22: 
23: // ─── Session ID extraction ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
24: // OpenCode SSE events store sessionID in different locations by event type:
25: //   - Top-level: message.part.delta, session.status, message.part.removed, etc.
26: //   - Nested in part: message.part.updated → properties.part.sessionID
27: //   - Nested in info: message.updated → properties.info.sessionID
28: // We must check all locations to correctly attribute events to sessions.
29: 
30: export function extractSessionId(event: OpenCodeEvent): string | undefined {
31: 	const props = event.properties;
32: 	// 1. Top-level sessionID (most common)
33: 	if (hasSessionID(props)) {
34: 		return props.sessionID;
35: 	}
36: 	// 2. Nested in part (message.part.updated)
37: 	if (hasPartWithSessionID(props)) {
38: 		return props.part.sessionID;
39: 	}
40: 	// 3. Nested in info (message.updated, session.updated)
41: 	if (hasInfoWithSessionID(props)) {
42: 		return props.info.sessionID ?? props.info.id;
43: 	}
44: 	return undefined;
45: }
46: 
47: // ─── Cache-eligible event types ──────────────────────────────────────────────
48: // Only chat-relevant events should be recorded to the message cache.
49: // Non-chat events (file_changed, permission_*, todo_state, etc.) would create
50: // false-positive cache hits that skip the REST API fallback on session switch,
51: // causing pre-relay conversation history to be lost.
52: 
53: const CACHEABLE_TYPES = new Set([
54: 	"user_message",
55: 	"delta",
56: 	"thinking_start",
57: 	"thinking_delta",
58: 	"thinking_stop",
59: 	"tool_start",
60: 	"tool_executing",
61: 	"tool_result",
62: 	"result",
63: 	"status",
64: 	"done",
65: 	"error",
66: ]);
67: 
68: export function isCacheable(msg: { type: string }): boolean {
69: 	return CACHEABLE_TYPES.has(msg.type);
70: }
71: 
72: // ─── SSE Wiring Dependencies ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
73: 
74: export interface SSEWiringDeps {
75: 	translator: Translator;
76: 	sessionMgr: SessionManager;
77: 	messageCache: MessageCache;
78: 	permissionBridge: PermissionBridge;
79: 	questionBridge: QuestionBridge;
80: 	overrides: SessionOverrides;
81: 	wsHandler: {
82: 		broadcast: (msg: RelayMessage) => void;
83: 	};
84: 	pushManager?: PushNotificationManager;
85: 	log: (...args: unknown[]) => void;
86: }
87: 
88: // ─── Handle a single SSE event ───────────────────────────────────────────────
89: 
90: export function handleSSEEvent(
91: 	deps: SSEWiringDeps,
92: 	event: OpenCodeEvent,
93: ): void {
94: 	const {
95: 		translator,
96: 		sessionMgr,
97: 		messageCache,
98: 		permissionBridge,
99: 		questionBridge,
100: 		overrides,
101: 		wsHandler,
102: 		pushManager,
103: 		log,
104: 	} = deps;
105: 
106: 	const eventSessionId = extractSessionId(event);
107: 	log(`   [sse] event=${event.type} session=${eventSessionId ?? "?"}`);
108: 
109: 	// ── Permission / question bridge routing ──────────────────────────────
110: 
111: 	if (event.type === "permission.asked") {
112: 		permissionBridge.onPermissionRequest(event);
113: 		if (pushManager) {
114: 			const id =
115: 				typeof event.properties.id === "string"
116: 					? event.properties.id
117: 					: "unknown";
118: 			const tool =
119: 				typeof event.properties.tool === "string"
120: 					? event.properties.tool
121: 					: typeof event.properties.permission === "string"
122: 						? event.properties.permission
123: 						: "A tool";
124: 			pushManager
125: 				.sendToAll({
126: 					type: "permission_request",
127: 					title: "Permission Needed",
128: 					body: `${tool} needs approval`,
129: 					tag: `perm-${id}`,
130: 				})
131: 				.catch(() => {});
132: 		}
133: 	}
134: 	if (event.type === "question.asked") {
135: 		questionBridge.onQuestion(event);
136: 		if (pushManager) {
137: 			pushManager
138: 				.sendToAll({
139: 					type: "ask_user",
140: 					title: "Question from Claude",
141: 					body: "Claude has a question for you",
142: 					tag: "opencode-ask",
143: 				})
144: 				.catch(() => {});
145: 		}
146: 	}
147: 	if (isPermissionRepliedEvent(event)) {
148: 		permissionBridge.onPermissionReplied(event.properties.id);
149: 	}
150: 
151: 	// ── Log session errors for debugging ──────────────────────────────────
152: 
153: 	if (isSessionErrorEvent(event)) {
154: 		const err = event.properties.error;
155: 		log(
156: 			`   [sse] event=${event.type} session=${eventSessionId ?? "?"} Session error: ${err?.name ?? "?"} — ${err?.data?.message ?? "(no message)"}`,
157: 		);
158: 	}
159: 
160: 	// ── Translate → filter → cache → broadcast ───────────────────────────
161: 
162: 	const messages = translator.translate(event);
163: 	if (!messages) return;
164: 
165: 	const activeId = sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId();
166: 	const isActiveSession = !eventSessionId || eventSessionId === activeId;
167: 
168: 	const toSend = Array.isArray(messages) ? messages : [messages];
169: 	for (const msg of toSend) {
170: 		// Clear processing timeout when done event arrives for active session
171: 		if (msg.type === "done" && isActiveSession) {
172: 			overrides.clearProcessingTimeout();
173: 		}
174: 
175: 		// Only record chat-relevant events to the cache
176: 		const recordId = eventSessionId ?? activeId;
177: 		if (recordId && isCacheable(msg)) {
178: 			messageCache.recordEvent(recordId, msg);
179: 		}
180: 
181: 		// Only broadcast events for the active session to browser clients
182: 		if (!isActiveSession) continue;
183: 		wsHandler.broadcast(msg);
184: 
185: 		// Push notifications for done/error events
186: 		if (pushManager) {
187: 			if (msg.type === "done") {
188: 				pushManager
189: 					.sendToAll({
190: 						type: "done",
191: 						title: "Task Complete",
192: 						body: "Claude has finished processing.",
193: 						tag: "opencode-done",
194: 					})
195: 					.catch(() => {});
196: 			}
197: 			if (msg.type === "error") {
198: 				pushManager
199: 					.sendToAll({
200: 						type: "error",
201: 						title: "Error",
202: 						body: msg.message || "An error occurred",
203: 						tag: "opencode-error",
204: 					})
205: 					.catch(() => {});
206: 			}
207: 		}
208: 	}
209: }
210: 
211: // ─── Wire all SSE consumer event listeners ───────────────────────────────────
212: 
213: export function wireSSEConsumer(
214: 	deps: SSEWiringDeps,
215: 	consumer: SSEConsumer,
216: ): void {
217: 	const { log } = deps;
218: 
219: 	consumer.on("connected", () =>
220: 		log(
221: 			`   [sse] session=${deps.sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId() ?? "?"} Connected to OpenCode event stream`,
222: 		),
223: 	);
224: 	consumer.on("disconnected", (err) =>
225: 		log(
226: 			`   [sse] session=${deps.sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId() ?? "?"} Disconnected${err ? `: ${err.message}` : ""}`,
227: 		),
228: 	);
229: 	consumer.on("reconnecting", ({ attempt, delay }) =>
230: 		log(
231: 			`   [sse] session=${deps.sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId() ?? "?"} Reconnecting (attempt ${attempt}, ${delay}ms delay)…`,
232: 		),
233: 	);
234: 	consumer.on("error", (err) =>
235: 		log(
236: 			`   [sse] session=${deps.sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId() ?? "?"} Error: ${err.message}`,
237: 		),
238: 	);
239: 
240: 	consumer.on("event", (event: OpenCodeEvent) => {
241: 		handleSSEEvent(deps, event);
242: 	});
243: }

(End of file - total 243 lines)

<content>1: // ─── WebSocket Store ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // Manages WebSocket connection lifecycle and centralized message dispatch.
3: // Replaces the handler registry pattern from vanilla modules.
4: 
5: import type { ConnectionStatus, RelayMessage } from "../types.js";
6: import {
7: 	addUserMessage,
8: 	chatState,
9: 	clearMessages,
10: 	flushPendingRender,
11: 	handleDelta,
12: 	handleDone,
13: 	handleError,
14: 	handleMessageRemoved,
15: 	handlePartRemoved,
16: 	handleResult,
17: 	handleStatus,
18: 	handleThinkingDelta,
19: 	handleThinkingStart,
20: 	handleThinkingStop,
21: 	handleToolExecuting,
22: 	handleToolResult,
23: 	handleToolStart,
24: } from "./chat.svelte.js";
25: import {
26: 	handleAgentList,
27: 	handleCommandList,
28: 	handleModelInfo,
29: 	handleModelList,
30: } from "./discovery.svelte.js";
31: import {
32: 	handleAskUser,
33: 	handleAskUserResolved,
34: 	handlePermissionRequest,
35: 	handlePermissionResolved,
36: } from "./permissions.svelte.js";
37: import { handleProjectList } from "./project.svelte.js";
38: import { handleSessionList, handleSessionSwitched } from "./session.svelte.js";
39: import {
40: 	handlePtyCreated,
41: 	handlePtyDeleted,
42: 	handlePtyError,
43: 	handlePtyExited,
44: 	handlePtyList,
45: 	handlePtyOutput,
46: } from "./terminal.svelte.js";
47: import { setClientCount, showBanner } from "./ui.svelte.js";
48: 
49: // ─── Re-exports from extracted modules ──────────────────────────────────────
50: // These were extracted for modularity but consumers still import from here.
51: 
52: export {
53: 	isPushActive,
54: 	setPushActive,
55: 	triggerNotifications,
56: } from "./ws-notifications.js";
57: 
58: import { triggerNotifications } from "./ws-notifications.js";
59: 
60: export {
61: 	fileBrowserListeners,
62: 	fileHistoryListeners,
63: 	historyPageListeners,
64: 	type MessageListener,
65: 	onFileBrowser,
66: 	onFileHistory,
67: 	onHistoryPage,
68: 	onPlanMode,
69: 	onProject,
70: 	onRewind,
71: 	planModeListeners,
72: 	projectListeners,
73: 	rewindListeners,
74: } from "./ws-listeners.js";
75: 
76: import {
77: 	fileBrowserListeners,
78: 	fileHistoryListeners,
79: 	historyPageListeners,
80: 	planModeListeners,
81: 	projectListeners,
82: 	rewindListeners,
83: } from "./ws-listeners.js";
84: 
85: // ─── State ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
86: 
87: let _ws: WebSocket | null = null;
88: let _reconnectTimer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null;
89: 
90: const RECONNECT_DELAY_MS = 2000;
91: 
92: export const wsState = $state({
93: 	status: "" as ConnectionStatus,
94: 	statusText: "",
95: });
96: 
97: // ─── Derived getters ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
98: 
99: /** Get whether the WebSocket is connected. */
100: export function getIsConnected(): boolean {
101: 	return wsState.status === "connected" || wsState.status === "processing";
102: }
103: 
104: // ─── Core functions ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
105: 
106: /** Send a JSON message over the WebSocket. */
107: export function wsSend(data: Record<string, unknown>): void {
108: 	if (_ws && _ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
109: 		_ws.send(JSON.stringify(data));
110: 	}
111: }
112: 
113: /** Set connection status with tooltip text. */
114: function setStatus(status: ConnectionStatus, text: string): void {
115: 	wsState.status = status;
116: 	wsState.statusText = text;
117: }
118: 
119: // ─── Connection lifecycle ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
120: 
121: /** Connect callbacks — called after connection established. */
122: let _onConnectFn: (() => void) | null = null;
123: let _onDisconnectFn: (() => void) | null = null;
124: 
125: export function onConnect(fn: () => void): void {
126: 	_onConnectFn = fn;
127: }
128: 
129: export function onDisconnect(fn: () => void): void {
130: 	_onDisconnectFn = fn;
131: }
132: 
133: /** Current slug — stored for auto-reconnect so it reconnects to the right project. */
134: let _currentSlug: string | undefined;
135: 
136: /** Establish WebSocket connection with auto-reconnect. */
137: export function connect(slug?: string): void {
138: 	_currentSlug = slug;
139: 
140: 	if (_ws) {
141: 		// Remove event listeners to prevent the old socket's close handler
142: 		// from interfering with the new connection (race condition fix).
143: 		const oldWs = _ws;
144: 		_ws = null;
145: 		oldWs.close();
146: 	}
147: 
148: 	if (_reconnectTimer) {
149: 		clearTimeout(_reconnectTimer);
150: 		_reconnectTimer = null;
151: 	}
152: 
153: 	const protocol = window.location.protocol === "https:" ? "wss:" : "ws:";
154: 	const path = slug ? `/p/${slug}/ws` : "/ws";
155: 	const url = `${protocol}//${window.location.host}${path}`;
156: 
157: 	const ws = new WebSocket(url);
158: 	_ws = ws;
159: 
160: 	ws.addEventListener("open", () => {
161: 		// Guard: only act if this is still the current socket
162: 		if (_ws !== ws) return;
163: 		setStatus("connected", "Connected");
164: 		_onConnectFn?.();
165: 	});
166: 
167: 	ws.addEventListener("close", () => {
168: 		// Guard: only act if this is still the current socket.
169: 		// If connect() was called again, _ws points to the new socket
170: 		// and we must not null it or start a reconnect timer.
171: 		if (_ws !== ws) return;
172: 
173: 		setStatus("disconnected", "Disconnected");
174: 		_ws = null;
175: 
176: 		// Reset chat streaming/processing state so UI isn't stuck
177: 		chatState.streaming = false;
178: 		chatState.processing = false;
179: 
180: 		_onDisconnectFn?.();
181: 
182: 		// Auto-reconnect (use stored slug so we reconnect to the right project)
183: 		if (_reconnectTimer) clearTimeout(_reconnectTimer);
184: 		_reconnectTimer = setTimeout(() => {
185: 			_reconnectTimer = null;
186: 			connect(_currentSlug);
187: 		}, RECONNECT_DELAY_MS);
188: 	});
189: 
190: 	ws.addEventListener("error", () => {
191: 		if (_ws !== ws) return;
192: 		setStatus("error", "Connection error");
193: 	});
194: 
195: 	ws.addEventListener("message", (event) => {
196: 		if (_ws !== ws) return;
197: 		try {
198: 			const msg = JSON.parse(event.data) as RelayMessage;
199: 			handleMessage(msg);
200: 		} catch {
201: 			console.warn("[ws] Failed to parse message:", event.data);
202: 		}
203: 	});
204: }
205: 
206: /** Disconnect and stop reconnecting. */
207: export function disconnect(): void {
208: 	if (_reconnectTimer) {
209: 		clearTimeout(_reconnectTimer);
210: 		_reconnectTimer = null;
211: 	}
212: 	if (_ws) {
213: 		_ws.close();
214: 		_ws = null;
215: 	}
216: 	setStatus("disconnected", "Disconnected");
217: }
218: 
219: // ─── Centralized message dispatch ───────────────────────────────────────────
220: 
221: /**
222:  * Route an incoming WebSocket message to the appropriate store handler.
223:  * Replaces the vanilla handler registry pattern.
224:  */
225: export function handleMessage(msg: RelayMessage): void {
226: 	switch (msg.type) {
227: 		// ─── Chat / Streaming ────────────────────────────────────────────
228: 		case "delta":
229: 			handleDelta(msg);
230: 			break;
231: 		case "thinking_start":
232: 			handleThinkingStart(msg);
233: 			break;
234: 		case "thinking_delta":
235: 			handleThinkingDelta(msg);
236: 			break;
237: 		case "thinking_stop":
238: 			handleThinkingStop(msg);
239: 			break;
240: 		case "tool_start":
241: 			handleToolStart(msg);
242: 			break;
243: 		case "tool_executing":
244: 			handleToolExecuting(msg);
245: 			break;
246: 		case "tool_result":
247: 			handleToolResult(msg);
248: 			break;
249: 		case "result":
250: 			handleResult(msg);
251: 			break;
252: 		case "done":
253: 			handleDone(msg);
254: 			triggerNotifications(msg);
255: 			break;
256: 		case "status":
257: 			handleStatus(msg);
258: 			break;
259: 		case "error":
260: 			handleChatError(msg);
261: 			triggerNotifications(msg);
262: 			break;
263: 
264: 		// ─── Sessions ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
265: 		case "session_list":
266: 			handleSessionList(msg);
267: 			break;
268: 		case "session_switched":
269: 			console.debug(
270: 				`[ws] session_switched id=${msg.id} events=${Array.isArray(msg.events) ? msg.events.length : "none"} history=${msg.history ? "yes" : "no"}`,
271: 			);
272: 			handleSessionSwitched(msg);
273: 			clearMessages();
274: 
275: 			// Reset HistoryView synchronously BEFORE dispatching new data.
276: 			// This replaces the $effect in HistoryView that was racing with
277: 			// handleHistoryPage (the $effect fired in a microtask AFTER data
278: 			// was loaded, wiping it).
279: 			for (const fn of historyPageListeners) {
280: 				fn({ type: "history_reset" });
281: 			}
282: 
283: 			if (msg.events) {
284: 				// Cache hit: replay raw events through existing chat handlers
285: 				// (full fidelity — same code paths as live streaming)
286: 				replayEvents(msg.events);
287: 			} else if (msg.history) {
288: 				// REST API fallback: dispatch structured messages to HistoryView
289: 				for (const fn of historyPageListeners) {
290: 					fn({
291: 						type: "history_page",
292: 						sessionId: msg.id,
293: 						messages: msg.history.messages,
294: 						hasMore: msg.history.hasMore,
295: 						total: msg.history.total,
296: 					});
297: 				}
298: 			}
299: 			break;
300: 
301: 		// ─── Terminal / PTY ──────────────────────────────────────────────
302: 		case "pty_list":
303: 			handlePtyList(msg);
304: 			break;
305: 		case "pty_created":
306: 			handlePtyCreated(msg);
307: 			break;
308: 		case "pty_output":
309: 			handlePtyOutput(msg);
310: 			break;
311: 		case "pty_exited":
312: 			handlePtyExited(msg);
313: 			break;
314: 		case "pty_deleted":
315: 			handlePtyDeleted(msg);
316: 			break;
317: 
318: 		// ─── Discovery ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
319: 		case "agent_list":
320: 			handleAgentList(msg);
321: 			break;
322: 		case "model_list":
323: 			handleModelList(msg);
324: 			break;
325: 		case "model_info":
326: 			handleModelInfo(msg);
327: 			break;
328: 		case "command_list":
329: 			handleCommandList(msg);
330: 			break;
331: 
332: 		// ─── Permissions & Questions ─────────────────────────────────────
333: 		case "permission_request":
334: 			handlePermissionRequest(msg, wsSend);
335: 			triggerNotifications(msg);
336: 			break;
337: 		case "permission_resolved":
338: 			handlePermissionResolved(msg);
339: 			break;
340: 		case "ask_user":
341: 			handleAskUser(msg);
342: 			triggerNotifications(msg);
343: 			break;
344: 		case "ask_user_resolved":
345: 			handleAskUserResolved(msg);
346: 			break;
347: 
348: 		// ─── UI ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
349: 		case "client_count":
350: 			setClientCount(msg.count ?? 0);
351: 			break;
352: 		case "banner":
353: 		case "skip_permissions":
354: 		case "update_available":
355: 			handleBannerMessage(msg);
356: 			break;
357: 
358: 		// ─── History ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
359: 		case "history_page":
360: 			// Handled by history component directly (not stored globally)
361: 			for (const fn of historyPageListeners) fn(msg);
362: 			break;
363: 
364: 		// ─── Plan Mode ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
365: 		case "plan_enter":
366: 		case "plan_exit":
367: 		case "plan_content":
368: 		case "plan_approval":
369: 			for (const fn of planModeListeners) fn(msg);
370: 			break;
371: 
372: 		// ─── File Browser ────────────────────────────────────────────────
373: 		case "file_list":
374: 		case "file_content":
375: 			for (const fn of fileBrowserListeners) fn(msg);
376: 			break;
377: 
378: 		// ─── File Changes (routed to both browser and history) ──────────
379: 		case "file_changed":
380: 			for (const fn of fileBrowserListeners) fn(msg);
381: 			for (const fn of fileHistoryListeners) fn(msg);
382: 			break;
383: 		case "file_history_result":
384: 			for (const fn of fileHistoryListeners) fn(msg);
385: 			break;
386: 
387: 		// ─── Rewind ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
388: 		case "rewind_result":
389: 			for (const fn of rewindListeners) fn(msg);
390: 			break;
391: 
392: 		// ─── Project ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
393: 		case "project_list":
394: 			handleProjectList(msg);
395: 			for (const fn of projectListeners) fn(msg);
396: 			break;
397: 
398: 		// ─── Todo ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
399: 		// Todo messages are handled as tool_start/tool_result
400: 		// (the TodoWrite tool result contains JSON parsed by the todo component)
401: 
402: 		// ─── Part / Message removal ──────────────────────────────────────
403: 		case "part_removed":
404: 			handlePartRemoved(msg);
405: 			break;
406: 		case "message_removed":
407: 			handleMessageRemoved(msg);
408: 			break;
409: 
410: 		default:
411: 			// Unknown message type — log for debugging
412: 			// Cast needed: import.meta.env is Vite-specific, not in standard tsconfig
413: 			if ((import.meta as { env?: { DEV?: boolean } }).env?.DEV) {
414: 				console.debug("[ws] Unhandled message type:", msg.type, msg);
415: 			}
416: 			break;
417: 	}
418: }
419: 
420: // ─── Event Replay (session switch with cached events) ────────────────────────
421: // Replays raw events through existing chat handlers — identical code paths
422: // as live streaming. Zero conversion, full fidelity, handles mid-stream.
423: 
424: function replayEvents(events: RelayMessage[]): void {
425: 	chatState.replaying = true;
426: 	for (const event of events) {
427: 		switch (event.type) {
428: 			case "user_message":
429: 				addUserMessage(event.text);
430: 				break;
431: 			case "delta":
432: 				handleDelta(event);
433: 				break;
434: 			case "done":
435: 				handleDone(event);
436: 				break;
437: 			case "tool_start":
438: 				handleToolStart(event);
439: 				break;
440: 			case "tool_executing":
441: 				handleToolExecuting(event);
442: 				break;
443: 			case "tool_result":
444: 				handleToolResult(event);
445: 				break;
446: 			case "result":
447: 				handleResult(event);
448: 				break;
449: 			case "thinking_start":
450: 				handleThinkingStart(event);
451: 				break;
452: 			case "thinking_delta":
453: 				handleThinkingDelta(event);
454: 				break;
455: 			case "thinking_stop":
456: 				handleThinkingStop(event);
457: 				break;
458: 			case "status":
459: 				handleStatus(event);
460: 				break;
461: 			case "error":
462: 				handleError(event);
463: 				break;
464: 			// Skip: session_switched, permission_*, session_list, etc.
465: 			// These are not part of the chat message stream.
466: 		}
467: 	}
468: 	// Flush any pending debounced render (for mid-stream sessions
469: 	// where no "done" event has been received yet)
470: 	flushPendingRender();
471: 	chatState.replaying = false;
472: }
473: 
474: // ─── Error handler (needs special routing) ──────────────────────────────────
475: 
476: function handleChatError(msg: Extract<RelayMessage, { type: "error" }>): void {
477: 	const code = msg.code;
478: 
479: 	// PTY-related errors
480: 	if (code === "PTY_CONNECT_FAILED" || code === "HANDLER_ERROR") {
481: 		handlePtyError(msg);
482: 		return;
483: 	}
484: 
485: 	// Chat errors
486: 	handleError(msg);
487: }
488: 
489: // ─── Banner handler ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
490: 
491: function handleBannerMessage(msg: RelayMessage): void {
492: 	switch (msg.type) {
493: 		case "update_available":
494: 			showBanner({
495: 				id: "update-available",
496: 				variant: "update",
497: 				icon: "arrow-up-circle",
498: 				text: `Update available: ${msg.version ?? "new version"}`,
499: 				dismissible: true,
500: 				version: msg.version,
501: 			});
502: 			break;
503: 		case "skip_permissions":
504: 			showBanner({
505: 				id: "skip-permissions",
506: 				variant: "skip-permissions",
507: 				icon: "shield-off",
508: 				text: "Permissions are being skipped",
509: 				dismissible: true,
510: 			});
511: 			break;
512: 		case "banner":
513: 			showBanner({
514: 				id: msg.config.id ?? "custom",
515: 				variant:
516: 					(msg.config.variant as
517: 						| "update"
518: 						| "onboarding"
519: 						| "skip-permissions") ?? "onboarding",
520: 				icon: msg.config.icon ?? "info",
521: 				text: msg.config.text ?? "",
522: 				dismissible: msg.config.dismissible ?? true,
523: 			});
524: 			break;
525: 	}
526: }
